title: Harmonic Pendulum Wave
description: A physics simulation of N coupled pendulums with monotonically increasing frequencies creating a synchronized wave pattern, featuring real-time parameter modulation (count, speed, amplitude, damping), rAF-driven trigonometric rendering, and cohesion telemetry.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Procedural frequency distribution: ω_i = 2π × baseFreq × (1 + i/N × 1.15) creating monotonically increasing angular velocities with periodic realignment
Hardware-accelerated rendering: translate3d(Xpx, 0, 0) applied to bob divs each frame via rAF loop, avoiding layout repaints
Real-time slider modulation: count slider rebuilds DOM + recalculates ω array, speed scales dt multiplier, amplitude scales A, damping applies exponential decay e^(-damping × t)
Cohesion telemetry: average pairwise sine difference tracked as 0-1 coefficient, displayed as gold value
Pause/Resume state machine: toggle between INITIATE MOTION and PAUSE with state indicator (RUNNING=green, PAUSED=yellow, IDLE=gray)
Reset pipeline: zeroes t, clears transforms, removes active glow classes, updates telemetry without page reload
Progressive glow effect: bob and string elements gain active class (brighter border + larger box-shadow bloom) when |x| > 2px
Frame delta clamping: dt capped at 0.05s to prevent spiral-of-death after tab-away
